mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is falling from a restrictive level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the sign flips and falling rates coincide with negative returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 10% signals severe labor-market stress, weakening household income expectations and risk appetite, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
failure_condition: Reject if post-crossing 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of UNRATE>10% episodes, or if the average return is not below the unconditional 5-day baseline by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broader demand stabilization through retail spending and housing activity, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings expectations broaden beyond financials.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60-day SPX returns do not rise when both RSAFS and HOUST improve together, or if either series alone explains the signal and the joint effect is not stronger than single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO after a contraction phase indicates a rebound in industrial output and cyclically sensitive revenues, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if two-month INDPRO rebounds are not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal is absent once monthly INDPRO growth turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Falling CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS held near zero implies disinflation without renewed tightening, which lowers real-rate pressure and supports equity multiples over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if falling CPI with near-zero FEDFUNDS does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if inflation declines only help equities when rates are also falling sharply.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS signals labor-market healing and rising household income capacity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if months with rising PAYEMS do not show higher subsequent 20-day SPX returns than falling-PAYEMS months, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the unemployment rate.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad demand contraction, reducing revenue growth and earnings revisions across sectors and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both decline in the latest available readings
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if only one of the two series matters and the joint condition adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is high and PAYEMS is still contracting, labor demand has not stabilized and household income prospects remain weak, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 5 trading days before any durable recovery begins.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains at or above 9.5% while PAYEMS is falling month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-unemployment and falling-payrolls condition is not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in most occurrences, or if the signal weakens to zero once either variable is included alone.
